title: Immigration
description: Municipality registration and residence permits after arriving in Belgium.

items.title: Municipality Registration
items.description: Non-EU nationals staying longer than 90 days must register their address at the local commune/gemeente within 8 working days of arrival, per Belgium's Federal Public Service (FPS) Home Affairs rules.
steps: Bring your passport with visa, lease contract (or a signed 'address hosting declaration' from your host if not renting), and passport-style ID photos
Attend the address verification visit from local police, typically scheduled after your initial registration
Collect your residence permit / registration card once approved — the full process takes around 2 months
tips: Missing the 8-working-day deadline can complicate banking, healthcare enrolment and future visa renewals
If you don't have a lease yet, arrange the host's signed address declaration before your appointment to avoid losing time against the deadline

items.title: Residence Permit
items.description: The permit type (e.g. Single Permit for work, student permit, family reunification) determines validity and renewal conditions.
tips: Start renewal applications well before expiry — processing can take several weeks to a couple of months
